title: Best VPN for China 2026: 6 VPNs That Actually Beat the Great Firewall
description: Find the best VPN for China in 2026. We tested which VPNs still bypass the Great Firewall — covering ExpressVPN, Astrill, NordVPN, Surfshark, and what you must do before you land.
content:
The best VPN for China in 2026 is ExpressVPN, followed by Astrill VPN and NordVPN with obfuscated servers enabled. China's Great Firewall blocks virtually every major Western website and app you rely on: Google, Gmail, YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and thousands more. A VPN routes your traffic through a server outside China, bypassing the firewall — but only specific VPNs with obfuscation technology actually work. Most VPNs fail within seconds of connecting from a Chinese IP address.

Why Most VPNs Fail in China

China's Great Firewall uses deep packet inspection (DPI) to identify and block VPN traffic in real time. Standard protocols like OpenVPN and WireGuard are recognizable by their traffic patterns and get blocked within minutes. The firewall also performs active probing, sending test traffic to suspected VPN servers to confirm their identity before blocking them.

VPNs that work in China disguise their traffic to look like ordinary HTTPS web browsing — a technique called obfuscation. Without it, your VPN connection drops within seconds.

The GFW also tightens significantly around politically sensitive periods: national holidays, anniversaries, and major political events. A VPN that works in March may struggle in October. This is why you must download and configure your VPN before arriving in China, not after.

The 6 Best VPNs for China in 2026

1. ExpressVPN — Best Overall

ExpressVPN consistently ranks as the top pick for China. Their Lightway protocol is fast and hard to detect. Obfuscated servers activate automatically when the app detects a restricted network environment.

Why it works: Lightway protocol with automatic obfuscation, servers optimized for China connections in Hong Kong, Japan, and Singapore, automatic server switching when a server gets blocked, 24/7 live chat support for troubleshooting.

Speed: Speed reduction of 14-25% in testing — acceptable for streaming and video calls.

Price: Around $6.67/month on a 12-month plan. 30-day money-back guarantee.

Critical note: ExpressVPN's website is blocked in China. Download and install the app before you travel.

2. Astrill VPN — Best for Long-Term Residents

Astrill has a cult following among expats living in China because it has a track record of keeping connections working even when other VPNs struggle. Their StealthVPN protocol is specifically maintained for China use cases.

Why it works: StealthVPN protocol designed to evade DPI, dedicated China servers updated regularly as the GFW evolves, App Guard feature that blocks apps from connecting if the VPN drops, split tunneling to route only specific apps through the VPN.

Speed: Fastest speeds of any VPN tested in China. HD video streaming without buffering is consistently achievable.

Price: Around $12.50/month — more expensive, but worth it for people who live or work in China long-term.

3. NordVPN — Best Value for Travelers

NordVPN works in China when you enable Obfuscated Servers, a setting not on by default. With it active, NordVPN shows 70-85% connection success rates on hotel and residential Wi-Fi in China.

How to set it up for China: Open NordVPN settings, go to Advanced, enable Obfuscated Servers, then connect to Hong Kong, Taiwan, or Japan.

Price: Around $3.99/month on a 2-year plan — the best value option for China travelers.

Limitation: Not ideal for long-term China residents who need consistent daily reliability. Occasional server switching required.

4. Surfshark — Best Budget Pick

Surfshark's Camouflage Mode disguises VPN traffic to look like standard HTTPS and activates automatically when you use OpenVPN. In testing, Surfshark successfully accessed all major blocked sites including Google, YouTube, and WhatsApp.

Price: As low as $2.19/month on a 2-year plan. Unlimited simultaneous connections means you can protect every device you travel with on one subscription.

Limitation: Slightly lower speeds than ExpressVPN and Astrill. Fine for HD streaming and video calls, occasional buffering on 4K.

5. VyprVPN — Built for Censorship

VyprVPN's Chameleon protocol was built specifically for countries with heavy internet censorship. It scrambles the VPN metadata that DPI scans look for. VyprVPN also owns and manages all its servers, meaning blocked servers are replaced faster than with providers using rented infrastructure.

Price: Around $5/month on annual plans.

6. Mullvad VPN — Best for Privacy

Mullvad's WireGuard with Shadowsocks proxy wrapping makes it effective at evading GFW detection. Shadowsocks was originally developed specifically to circumvent Chinese censorship. Flat $5/month pricing, no annual contracts required.

Limitation: More technical setup than consumer VPNs. Recommended for tech-comfortable users.

VPNs That Do NOT Work in China

  • PIA (Private Internet Access): No obfuscation protocol. Blocked almost immediately.
  • CyberGhost: Blocked. Support acknowledges China is not a supported use case.
  • TunnelBear: Unreliable, especially during enforcement crackdowns.
  • Free VPNs: All blocked without exception. Many free VPNs active in China log your traffic.
  • Standard WireGuard or OpenVPN: Detectable and blocked by DPI.

Before You Travel to China: Critical Checklist

Follow this before you land — you cannot fix this once you're inside China:

  1. Download and install your VPN on all devices you're bringing
  2. Test the connection from your home country to confirm it works
  3. Enable obfuscation settings in the app before you travel
  4. Save your provider's support email address (their website will be blocked)
  5. Install a backup VPN — if your primary fails during a crackdown, a second option is invaluable

What Sites Are Blocked in China

As of 2026, these services are blocked inside China: Google Search, Gmail, Google Drive, Google Maps, YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter/X, WhatsApp, Telegram, Netflix, Twitch, BBC News, The New York Times, Slack, Discord, and most international news sites.

Is Using a VPN in China Legal?

China's government bans unauthorized VPNs. However, enforcement against individual tourists and business travelers is extremely rare. The law primarily targets VPN providers and Chinese citizens who distribute VPN software. Foreigners traveling to China routinely use VPNs with no consequences. The practical risk for a short-term traveler is low, but not technically zero.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which VPN works best in China right now?

ExpressVPN and Astrill are the most consistently reliable in 2026. ExpressVPN is the better pick for travelers who want easy setup. Astrill is preferred by long-term residents and expats who need daily reliability.

Do I need to download the VPN before entering China?

Yes, without exception. VPN provider websites are blocked in China. You cannot download or activate a new VPN once inside the country. Install and test before you travel.

Will a free VPN work in China?

No. Free VPNs lack the obfuscation technology to bypass the Great Firewall. Many free VPNs claiming to work in China are operated by entities with Chinese government connections and should not be trusted for sensitive communications.

Is WireGuard blocked in China?

Standard WireGuard is detectable and blocked by DPI. VPNs using WireGuard with an obfuscation layer on top (like Mullvad's Shadowsocks implementation) can work, but bare WireGuard connections fail.

How do I stay connected if my VPN stops working mid-trip?

Switch servers first, preferably to Hong Kong, Taiwan, or Japan endpoints. If that fails, switch protocols within the app. Contact your VPN's support via email if still failing. Having a second VPN installed is the most reliable backup.

Which VPN is best for streaming Netflix or YouTube from China?

ExpressVPN and Astrill deliver the fastest speeds and most consistent streaming performance. Surfshark works for HD streaming. NordVPN streams reliably on uncongested obfuscated servers.

Why does my VPN get slower during Chinese holidays?

China intensifies firewall enforcement during politically sensitive periods including National Day (October 1), Tiananmen anniversary (June 4), and major party congresses. VPNs that work normally may become intermittent. Astrill has the strongest track record during these enforcement windows.
